response.justification: Wei Chen holds genuine staff-level credentials and has directly relevant AI-agent and MCP infrastructure experience at re:cinq, which aligns well with the role's AI-native engineering baseline. Past work at Dunnhumby demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems chops (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency microservices). However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, which is a hard gap that cannot be assumed away. The distributed systems depth is also several years stale, and the resume offers limited evidence of stakeholder management, technical strategy ownership, or operational readiness. A brief screening call to probe the Python gap and validate the depth of current AI-native work is warranted before advancing.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq includes hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling work, directly satisfying the AI-native engineering baseline the role treats as table stakes

Dunnhumby experience demonstrates high-throughput distributed systems credibility — millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers, low-latency caching — mapping directly to the core technical requirements

Consistent staff-level titles across multiple employers, reinforced by open-source project ownership, public talks, and community workshop facilitation that signal technical leadership beyond pure IC work
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume, skills list, and all project descriptions — this is a stated must-have skill and represents a critical unresolved gap

High-throughput distributed systems depth is concentrated in a role from 2018–2020; subsequent and current positions are more product/full-stack in character, raising questions about how active that expertise remains
No explicit evidence of stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination, or operational ownership such as monitoring and on-call — key non-technical requirements that the role weights heavily
